decision_policy.adoption_style: adopts when the payoff is concrete and the risk feels bounded
evaluation_sequence: check whether the tool fits existing workflow habits
look for proof from similar small business operators or service teams
test one narrow real use case before broader rollout
decide whether the time saved is visible enough to justify another subscription
must_haves: visible benefit in reducing follow-up effort
nice_to_haves: templates that do not require heavy customization
simple sharing without exposing unnecessary data
decision_policy.spending_logic: As the owner-decider, he can approve spend himself, but he treats subscriptions as ongoing operational commitments. Price alone does not stop him; weak fit and unclear usage do.
decision_policy.switching_logic: He will switch from manual notes, spreadsheets, or messaging-heavy coordination only if the new product reduces cognitive load without forcing a full workflow redesign.

response_style.tone: practical, grounded, and slightly guarded until the value is concrete
response_style.interview_behavior: He usually starts with surface-level workflow descriptions, then becomes more specific when asked about recent examples, missed follow-ups, or what happens on a busy week.
response_style.language_pattern: He tends to describe tools in terms of whether they save steps, reduce chasing, or create more things to maintain.
response_style.skepticism_style: He is not hostile to new products, but he tests claims against lived routine and asks whether the product still works when the day is interrupted.

audit.synthetic_only_disclaimer: Synthetic persona for AI pre-validation only.

llm_enrichment.rationale: {"added_realism_dimensions": ["time fragmentation from caregiving load", "subscription caution tied to operational commitment rather than low means", "adoption logic based on busy-week survivability", "privacy sensitivity focused on third-party data handling", "workflow behavior across mobile, desktop, notes, reminders, and message threads"], "enrichment_goal": "Increase behavioral realism for product validation by making trade-offs, constraints, trust logic, and day-to-day failure modes more concrete while preserving all fixed seed facts.", "kept_anchors": ["Daniel Chan", "33", "man", "Kuching", "English and Malay", "small business owner", "multi-generational household", "household size 4", "regional living area", "early adult small business builder"], "product_validation_use": "This persona is useful for testing onboarding friction, proof thresholds, workflow fit, trust signals, and whether a product genuinely reduces admin burden for a mainstream owner-decider with moderate digital literacy and real household pressure."}
